rationale: The proof is mathematically correct and clearly framed via the open-cover characterisation, but it offers no calibrated confidence markers, assumption ledger, or discussion of edge cases (e.g., subspace vs. ambient cover formulations, Hausdorff hypotheses, or alternative proof routes), which a domain expert would flag as incomplete under this rubric.
rubric_evidence: let {U_i} be any open cover of F
Since F is closed, X\F is open
Thus every open cover of F has a finite subcover, so F is compact.
